not going to go to those restaurants. That's like, okay, I got your list, now I'm going to go, make you go places you don't want to go. This is what's going to happen. So, here's the magic. The first part is the same, I don't care. Here's the second part, in this case. You know which ones I like. You pick. Magic. The only thing that changes is which one on which day. It's worked for three years. We go to exactly the same restaurants every single time and they're the ones I like. But she picked them and she moves them around on different days, so she picked. It's perfect. It's just perfect. If I had figured this out, this is one of the, about 10 things. If I had figured this out years earlier, I probably could have avoided a divorce. See, I used to do it the other way. And then there's a big fight, right? Well, I told you where I wanted to go. Why the hell are we going these places? Because I don't want to go where you want to go. I mean, this works. So the hijack is to shove somebody into a situation where they think they're picking whatever it is you got, but you pre-framed the pick. They still think they're picking. The other version of this is you switch somebody from, do I want to do this or not? Am I interested in this or not? To which of these should I get? So when I sold at all the Peter Loew events, I sold an AB offer. So how many of you were at? How many of you, that's your origin? Those events, okay, it falls off year by year. But so for those of you who never saw them, that these big sports arenas, 10,000, 15,000 people, Zigg, Brian Tracy, me, with celebrities in between, we're selling stuff. So I sold an AB offer, $99, 278. In some other environments, three, but there I didn't have enough time. So one reason was to get people to the table for the lower threshold, the 99 bucks. And then there was a switch. Switch, by the way, had to be easy. You had to be able to teach it to temp. Right, so the switch was fine, happy to have this. This will be shipped to you. Or we have the complete package here today that you can take home with you. Would you like to upgrade? That was the switch, okay? So that switched like half of the $99 buyers. They just immediately, they took the bag and off they went on their merry way. But the other reason it was there is because people now would come, so they all gather, the ones that aren't buying are in a clump around me. And here's the top question. There was always some statement. I own a bakery. I just got business yesterday. I'm poor. Sydney, I had one, only one in all those years. I had one. I run an escort service. Do you know what that is? Yeah, I know what that is. I'm an adult male. But there was some statement about what they do. And then they would ask, which program should I get? Now, if I'd only had the 278, they would have left. Never would have asked the question. Now, which program should I get? It should come as no surprise to you. That in 99% of the cases, the thing they needed was the 278. Ever once in a while, over 10 years, I actually broke down. You know, it'd be like, I'm really poor and I'm sleeping in my car even I have some level of compunction. But mostly the answer was, well, you need to complete system. Okay, off they go. So you always want to switch, should I do this or not? To which one should I do? Should I do this or not? To which one should I get? If I am selling high-priced services to a new client, I almost never propose one prescription. I almost always put two prescriptions on the table. You could do this, you could do this. And inevitably, well, which one do you think I should do? In most of the cases, you should do the big one. That's what you should do, a big ass one. Very seldom do I say, well, I think you should do it all. But see, they feel like this was a collaborative process over which they had some control, right? They're no control. I picked both. Either one was acceptable to me, right? I framed the whole pick, but they feel like they picked. They go home and they say to spouse, I picked. Right? I decided, I chose. Well, he said I could do this or I could do that. I did this. Here's why I did this. They feel like they picked. And how they feel controls all sorts of things. People get sucked in by comparative choice all the time. And there's three things that affect comparative choice. These three things are not in your manual, so you might want to jot them down. One are choices of items. You're gonna get just the mightest touch system or you're gonna get mightest touch plus magnetic marketing. That's choice of item. Choice of timing. You're gonna do it now. You're gonna do it spread out over six months. Right? Differential benefit. There's two things you can do. In one, here's the benefits. In another, here's more benefits. Those are the three ways basically that we manipulate comparative choice. So here's a great instructive story. By the way, who knows what time we're supposed to be done? Five o'clock, thank you very much. It's a good year here. So when the home bread making machine was first introduced, it's one of those appliances nobody needs, right? When the home, I mean really. It's like, it's every once in a while somebody invites me to go fishing. I'm going, there's a supermarket. What are we living in, 1812? There's a grocery store over here. You know? So when the home bread making machine was first introduced and displayed in the Williams Sonoma stores, they had $270,000. Sales were virtually non-existent. And the product was on the verge of cancellation in Williams Sonoma. When a marketing guy convinced the manufacturer to create and convinced Williams Sonoma to display a second model of the machine next to the other model. The second model was bigger, a little fancier, and priced at $400. Five by five. Sales skyrocketed. Here's what happened. When there was one machine, here was your thought. A, what the hell is a bread maker? All right? And do I want a bread maker? No. Here's what the big ass one next to the little one did, right? I don't know much about bread makers, but I'd rather have the smaller one that takes less counter space. And whoop! Person instantly switched from, I don't know what a bread maker for, to I don't want that big ass one, I'll take the small one. Sales through the roof. Neil Strauss says in the game book, I told you to read.
